steps.title: Recognize what 'generalization' requires
steps.content: A “generalization” is a broad claim about the archive itself, not a specific fact about a single item.

steps.title: Identify the broad claim
steps.content: Choice C synthesizes information from the notes—the two different dates (1881 and 1915) for different artifacts—to make a generalization about the archive's temporal scope. Choices A, B, and D each report a single specific fact from the notes. That matches choice C — C is correct.
